# PO Schema Unification Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Unify the PO document schema into a single source of truth in `document_type_schema_versions`, wire the content builder to read from it, and add auto-generated Sections 1 and 10 to docgen.

**Architecture:** The service layer resolves `ContentSchema` from `document_type_schema_versions` instead of `document_profile_schema_versions`. All downstream schema readers (`resolveActiveProfileSchema`, `resolveDocumentProfileSchema`, editor bundle, profile bundle, runtime bundle) call `ListDocumentProfileSchemas` internally, so the single wiring change propagates everywhere. Docgen renders Sections 1 (Identificação) and 10 (Histórico de Revisões) from metadata passed in an extended payload. The footer is updated to read from payload metadata instead of `values.identificacao`.

**Tech Stack:** Go 1.24, PostgreSQL 16, TypeScript/Node (docx-js), React/Zustand frontend

---

## File Structure

| File | Action | Responsibility |
|------|--------|----------------|
| `migrations/0053_insert_po_v3_schema.sql` | Create | Insert PO v3 schema, activate it |
| `scripts/cleanup_test_documents.sql` | Create | Standalone cleanup script for test data (not a migration) |
| `internal/modules/documents/application/service_registry.go` | Modify | Resolve `ContentSchema` from type schemas in `ListDocumentProfileSchemas` |
| `internal/modules/documents/application/service.go` | Modify | Add workflow service dependency to `Service` struct |
| `internal/platform/render/docgen/types.go` | Modify | Add `Metadata` and `Revisions` fields to `RenderPayload` |
| `internal/modules/documents/application/service_document_runtime.go` | Modify | Populate metadata and revisions when building docgen payload |
| `apps/docgen/src/runtime/types.ts` | Modify | Add `metadata` and `revisions` to `DocumentPayload` |
| `apps/docgen/src/generate.ts` | Modify | Render auto-generated Sections 1 and 10, update footer |

### Task 1: Migration — PO v3 schema (additive only)

**Files:**
- Create: `migrations/0053_insert_po_v3_schema.sql`
- Create: `scripts/cleanup_test_documents.sql`

- [ ] **Step 1: Create the additive migration file**

This migration only inserts schema data. No destructive operations.

```sql
-- 0053_insert_po_v3_schema.sql
-- Insert PO v3 schema with 8 editable sections (2-9).
-- Sections 1 (Identificação) and 10 (Histórico de Revisões) are auto-generated by docgen.

INSERT INTO metaldocs.document_type_schema_versions (type_key, version, schema_json, governance_json)
VALUES (
  'po',
  3,
  $${
  "sections": [
    {
      "key": "identificacaoProcesso",
      "num": "2",
      "title": "Identificação do Processo",
      "color": "#0F6E56",
      "fields": [
        { "key": "objetivo", "label": "Objetivo", "type": "textarea", "required": true },
        { "key": "escopo", "label": "Escopo", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "responsavel", "label": "Cargo responsável", "type": "text" },
        { "key": "canal", "label": "Canal / Contexto", "type": "text" },
        { "key": "participantes", "label": "Participantes", "type": "textarea" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "entradasSaidas",
      "num": "3",
      "title": "Entradas e Saídas",
      "color": "#0F6E56",
      "fields": [
        { "key": "entradas", "label": "Entradas", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "saidas", "label": "Saídas", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "documentos", "label": "Documentos relacionados", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "sistemas", "label": "Sistemas utilizados", "type": "textarea" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "visaoGeral",
      "num": "4",
      "title": "Visão Geral do Processo",
      "color": "#BA7517",
      "fields": [
        { "key": "descricaoProcesso", "label": "Descrição do processo", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "fluxogramaFerramenta", "label": "Ferramenta do fluxograma", "type": "text" },
        { "key": "fluxogramaUrl", "label": "Link do fluxograma", "type": "text" },
        { "key": "fluxogramaDiagrama", "label": "Diagrama", "type": "rich" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "etapas",
      "num": "5",
      "title": "Detalhamento das Etapas",
      "color": "#993C1D",
      "fields": [
        {
          "key": "etapas",
          "label": "Etapas",
          "type": "repeat",
          "itemFields": [
            { "key": "num", "label": "Número", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "titulo", "label": "Título", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "responsavel", "label": "Responsável", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "prazo", "label": "Prazo / SLA", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "descricao", "label": "Descrição", "type": "rich" },
            { "key": "observacao", "label": "Observações", "type": "textarea" },
            { "key": "alerta", "label": "Alertas / Desvios", "type": "textarea" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "controle",
      "num": "6",
      "title": "Controle e Exceções",
      "color": "#0F6E56",
      "fields": [
        { "key": "pontosControle", "label": "Pontos de controle", "type": "textarea" },
        { "key": "excecoes", "label": "Exceções e desvios", "type": "textarea" }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "kpis",
      "num": "7",
      "title": "Indicadores de Desempenho",
      "color": "#0F6E56",
      "fields": [
        {
          "key": "kpis",
          "label": "KPIs",
          "type": "table",
          "columns": [
            { "key": "indicador", "label": "Indicador / KPI", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "meta", "label": "Meta", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "frequencia", "label": "Frequência", "type": "text" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "referencias",
      "num": "8",
      "title": "Documentos e Referências",
      "color": "#185FA5",
      "fields": [
        {
          "key": "referencias",
          "label": "Referências",
          "type": "table",
          "columns": [
            { "key": "codigo", "label": "Código", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "titulo", "label": "Título / Descrição", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "url", "label": "Link / Localização", "type": "text" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "key": "glossario",
      "num": "9",
      "title": "Glossário",
      "color": "#185FA5",
      "fields": [
        {
          "key": "glossario",
          "label": "Glossário",
          "type": "table",
          "columns": [
            { "key": "termo", "label": "Termo", "type": "text" },
            { "key": "definicao", "label": "Definição", "type": "text" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}$$::jsonb,
  '{}'::jsonb
)
ON CONFLICT (type_key, version) DO UPDATE
SET schema_json = EXCLUDED.schema_json;

UPDATE metaldocs.document_types SET active_version = 3 WHERE type_key = 'po';
```

### Task 2: Service layer — Resolve ContentSchema from type schemas

All downstream schema readers (`resolveActiveProfileSchema`, `resolveDocumentProfileSchema`, `GetDocumentEditorBundle`, `GetDocumentProfileBundle`, `GetDocumentRuntimeBundle`, save/export validation) call `ListDocumentProfileSchemas` internally. Changing this single method propagates the type schema to all paths.

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/modules/documents/application/service_registry.go:81-91`

- [ ] **Step 1: Modify `ListDocumentProfileSchemas` to resolve type schema**

In `internal/modules/documents/application/service_registry.go`, replace the `ListDocumentProfileSchemas` method:

```go
func (s *Service) ListDocumentProfileSchemas(ctx context.Context, profileCode string) ([]domain.DocumentProfileSchemaVersion, error) {
	profileCode = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(profileCode))
	items, err := s.repo.ListDocumentProfileSchemas(ctx, profileCode)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if len(items) == 0 {
		return filterDefaultSchemas(profileCode), nil
	}

	// Resolve ContentSchema from document_type_schema_versions if a matching type exists.
	// This is the single point where profile schemas are enriched with the type schema.
	// All downstream consumers (resolveActiveProfileSchema, resolveDocumentProfileSchema,
	// editor bundle, profile bundle, runtime bundle, export) call this method.
	if profileCode != "" {
		typeDef, err := s.repo.GetDocumentTypeDefinition(ctx, profileCode)
		if err == nil && typeDef.Schema.Sections != nil {
			schemaMap, marshalErr := typeSchemaToMap(typeDef.Schema)
			if marshalErr == nil {
				for i := range items {
					items[i].ContentSchema = schemaMap
				}
			}
		}
		// If type not found or marshal fails, fall back to profile's own content_schema_json (no-op).
	}

	return items, nil
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the `typeSchemaToMap` helper function**

Add this function at the bottom of `service_registry.go`, before the `filterDefaultSchemas` function:

```go
func typeSchemaToMap(schema domain.DocumentTypeSchema) (map[string]any, error) {
	raw, err := json.Marshal(schema)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	var out map[string]any
	if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &out); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return out, nil
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Add `encoding/json` import if not present**

Check the imports in `service_registry.go`. If `"encoding/json"` is not already imported, add it.

- [ ] **Step 4: Verify the Go code compiles**

Run: `cd internal && go build ./...`

Expected: No compilation errors.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit**

```bash
git add internal/modules/documents/application/service_registry.go
git commit -m "feat(schema): resolve ContentSchema from type schemas in ListDocumentProfileSchemas"
```

The documents `Service` needs access to workflow approvals and user display names for the docgen metadata. Currently it has neither.

**Files:**

	ResolveDisplayName(ctx context.Context, userID string) (string, error)
}

// WorkflowApprovalReader reads approval records for a document.
type WorkflowApprovalReader interface {
	ListApprovals(ctx context.Context, documentID string) ([]domain.ApprovalSummary, error)
}

}

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the `ApprovalSummary` domain type**

In `internal/modules/documents/domain/model.go`, add a minimal type used by the documents module (avoids importing workflow domain):

- [ ] **Step 3: Create adapters and wire in `main.go`**

In `apps/api/cmd/metaldocs-api/main.go`, the documents service is built at line 69. After adding the interfaces, create simple adapters that wrap existing dependencies and inject them.

Create a thin adapter file `internal/modules/documents/application/adapters.go`:

```go
package application

import (
	"context"

	workflowdomain "metaldocs/internal/modules/workflow/domain"
)

// WorkflowApprovalAdapter adapts workflow.ApprovalRepository to documents.WorkflowApprovalReader.
type WorkflowApprovalAdapter struct {
	repo workflowdomain.ApprovalRepository
}

func NewWorkflowApprovalAdapter(repo workflowdomain.ApprovalRepository) *WorkflowApprovalAdapter {
	return &WorkflowApprovalAdapter{repo: repo}
}

func (a *WorkflowApprovalAdapter) ListApprovals(ctx context.Context, documentID string) ([]domain.ApprovalSummary, error) {
	approvals, err := a.repo.ListApprovals(ctx, documentID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	out := make([]domain.ApprovalSummary, len(approvals))
	for i, a := range approvals {
		out[i] = domain.ApprovalSummary{
			ApproverID: a.DecisionBy,
			Status:     a.Status,
			DecidedAt:  a.DecidedAt,
		}
	}
	return out, nil
}
```

For user display name resolution, create a simple adapter that queries the IAM users table. Since the documents repo already has DB access, add a method to the existing repository, or use a standalone query adapter. The simplest approach: pass user IDs as-is for now, and add a `UserDisplayNameAdapter` in a follow-up. The `resolveUserDisplayName` helper already handles `nil` resolver gracefully.

In `apps/api/cmd/metaldocs-api/main.go`, wire the approval adapter:

```go
docService := docapp.NewService(deps.DocumentsRepo, deps.Publisher, nil).
    WithAttachmentStore(deps.AttachmentStore).
    WithAuditWriter(deps.AuditWriter).
    WithCarbone(deps.CarboneClient, deps.CarboneTemplates).
    WithDocgenClient(deps.DocgenClient).
    WithApprovalReader(docapp.NewWorkflowApprovalAdapter(deps.WorkflowApprovals))
```

Note: `WithUserResolver` is NOT wired yet — the `resolveUserDisplayName` helper falls back to returning the raw `userID` when `s.userResolver == nil`. This is acceptable for now; a `UserDisplayNameAdapter` can be added later.

### Task 4: Extend docgen payload with metadata and revisions

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/platform/render/docgen/types.go`
- Modify: `apps/docgen/src/runtime/types.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Rewrite Go `types.go` with new fields**

Replace the full content of `internal/platform/render/docgen/types.go`:

```go
package docgen

type RenderPayload struct {
	DocumentType string           `json:"documentType"`
	DocumentCode string           `json:"documentCode"`
	Title        string           `json:"title"`
	Version      string           `json:"version,omitempty"`
	Status       string           `json:"status,omitempty"`
	Schema       RenderSchema     `json:"schema"`
	Values       map[string]any   `json:"values"`
	Metadata     *RenderMetadata  `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
	Revisions    []RenderRevision `json:"revisions,omitempty"`
}

type RenderMetadata struct {
	ElaboradoPor string `json:"elaboradoPor"`
	AprovadoPor  string `json:"aprovadoPor"`
	CreatedAt    string `json:"createdAt"`
	ApprovedAt   string `json:"approvedAt"`
}

type RenderRevision struct {
	Versao    string `json:"versao"`
	Data      string `json:"data"`
	Descricao string `json:"descricao"`
	Por       string `json:"por"`
}

type RenderSchema struct {
	Sections []RenderSection `json:"sections"`
}

type RenderSection struct {
	Key    string        `json:"key"`
	Num    string        `json:"num"`
	Title  string        `json:"title"`
	Color  string        `json:"color,omitempty"`
	Fields []RenderField `json:"fields"`
}

type RenderField struct {
	Key        string        `json:"key"`
	Label      string        `json:"label"`
	Type       string        `json:"type"`
	Options    []string      `json:"options,omitempty"`
	Columns    []RenderField `json:"columns,omitempty"`
	ItemFields []RenderField `json:"itemFields,omitempty"`
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update TypeScript types**

In `apps/docgen/src/runtime/types.ts`, add the new interfaces before `DocumentPayload` and extend the payload:

```typescript
export interface DocumentMetadata {
  elaboradoPor: string;
  aprovadoPor: string;
  createdAt: string;
  approvedAt: string;
}

export interface DocumentRevision {
  versao: string;
  data: string;
  descricao: string;
  por: string;
}

export interface DocumentPayload {
  documentType: string;
  documentCode: string;
  title: string;
  version?: string;
  status?: string;
  schema: DocumentTypeSchema;
  values: DocumentValues;
  metadata?: DocumentMetadata;
  revisions?: DocumentRevision[];
}
```

### Task 5: Populate metadata and revisions in the Go service

**Files:**
- Modify: `internal/modules/documents/application/service_document_runtime.go:144-168`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add helper methods to `service_document_runtime.go`**

Add these methods after the existing `ExportDocumentDocxAuthorized` method:

```go
func (s *Service) resolveUserDisplayName(ctx context.Context, userID string) string {
	if userID == "" {
		return "\u2014"
	}
	if s.userResolver == nil {
		return userID
	}
	name, err := s.userResolver.ResolveDisplayName(ctx, userID)
	if err != nil || name == "" {
		return userID
	}
	return name
}

func (s *Service) resolveLatestApproval(ctx context.Context, documentID string) (approverName string, approvedAt string) {
	if s.approvalReader == nil {
		return "\u2014", ""
	}
	approvals, err := s.approvalReader.ListApprovals(ctx, documentID)
	if err != nil || len(approvals) == 0 {
		return "\u2014", ""
	}
	for i := len(approvals) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
		a := approvals[i]
		if strings.EqualFold(a.Status, "APPROVED") && a.DecidedAt != nil {
			name := s.resolveUserDisplayName(ctx, a.ApproverID)
			return name, a.DecidedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
		}
	}
	return "\u2014", ""
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Modify `ExportDocumentDocxAuthorized` to populate metadata and revisions**

Replace the `ExportDocumentDocxAuthorized` method:

```go
func (s *Service) ExportDocumentDocxAuthorized(ctx context.Context, documentID, traceID string) ([]byte, error) {
	if s.docgenClient == nil {
		return nil, domain.ErrRenderUnavailable
	}

	bundle, err := s.GetDocumentRuntimeBundle(ctx, documentID)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	schema, err := toDocgenSchema(bundle.Schema.ContentSchema)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	payload := docgen.RenderPayload{
		DocumentType: firstNonEmpty(bundle.Document.DocumentType, bundle.Document.DocumentProfile),
		DocumentCode: bundle.Document.DocumentCode,
		Title:        bundle.Document.Title,
		Version:      fmt.Sprintf("%d", bundle.Version.Number),
		Status:       bundle.Document.Status,
		Schema:       schema,
		Values:       cloneRuntimeValues(bundle.Version.Values),
	}

	// Populate metadata for auto-generated Section 1 (Identificação)
	ownerName := s.resolveUserDisplayName(ctx, bundle.Document.OwnerID)
	approverName, approvedAt := s.resolveLatestApproval(ctx, documentID)
	payload.Metadata = &docgen.RenderMetadata{
		ElaboradoPor: ownerName,
		AprovadoPor:  approverName,
		CreatedAt:    bundle.Document.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02"),
		ApprovedAt:   approvedAt,
	}

	// Populate revisions for auto-generated Section 10 (Histórico de Revisões)
	// Version struct has no CreatedBy field — use document owner for all versions.
	versions, err := s.repo.ListVersions(ctx, documentID)
	if err == nil && len(versions) > 0 {
		revisions := make([]docgen.RenderRevision, 0, len(versions))
		for _, v := range versions {
			summary := v.ChangeSummary
			if summary == "" && v.Number == 1 {
				summary = "Criação do documento"
			}
			revisions = append(revisions, docgen.RenderRevision{
				Versao:    fmt.Sprintf("%d", v.Number),
				Data:      v.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02"),
				Descricao: summary,
				Por:       ownerName,
			})
		}
		payload.Revisions = revisions
	}

	return s.docgenClient.Generate(ctx, payload, traceID)
}
```

Expected: No compilation errors.

### Task 6: Docgen — Render auto-generated Section 1 (Identificação) and update footer

**No duplication risk:** Sections 1 and 10 are NOT in the editable schema (`schema.sections` contains only `num: "2"` through `num: "9"`). The generic `runtime.schema.sections.flatMap(...)` renderer will never see them. The custom `buildIdentificationSection` and `buildRevisionHistorySection` are called separately in `generateDocx`.

**Files:**
- Modify: `apps/docgen/src/generate.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the import to include new types**

```typescript
import type {
  ColumnDef,
  DocumentMetadata,
  DocumentPayload,
  DocumentRevision,
  DocumentTypeSchema,
  DocumentValues,
  FieldDef,
  ScalarFieldType,
  SectionDef,
} from "./runtime/types.js";
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add `buildIdentificationSection` function**

Add after `buildFooter`:

```typescript
function buildIdentificationSection(runtime: DocumentPayload): (Paragraph | Table)[] {
  const meta = runtime.metadata;
  if (!meta) return [];

  const sectionColor = C.purple;
  const lightColor = C.purpleLight;

  const heading = makeTable(
    [
      new TableRow({
        children: [
          makeCell({
            width: CONTENT_WIDTH,
            fill: sectionColor,
            children: [
              paragraph([run("1  \u2014  IDENTIFICA\u00C7\u00C3O", { bold: true, color: C.white })]),
            ],
          }),
        ],
      }),
    ],
    [CONTENT_WIDTH],
    { width: CONTENT_WIDTH, borders: tableBorder(BorderStyle.NONE) },
  );

  const row1 = makeTable(
    [
      new TableRow({
        children: [
          makeCell({ width: 2200, fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Elaborado por", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2480, children: [paragraph([run(meta.elaboradoPor)])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2200, fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Aprovado por", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2480, children: [paragraph([run(meta.aprovadoPor)])] }),
        ],
      }),
    ],
    [2200, 2480, 2200, 2480],
    { width: CONTENT_WIDTH, borders: tableBorder(BorderStyle.NONE) },
  );

  const row2 = makeTable(
    [
      new TableRow({
        children: [
          makeCell({ width: 2200, fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Data de cria\u00E7\u00E3o", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2480, children: [paragraph([run(meta.createdAt)])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2200, fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Data de aprova\u00E7\u00E3o", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
          makeCell({ width: 2480, children: [paragraph([run(meta.approvedAt || "\u2014")])] }),
        ],
      }),
    ],
    [2200, 2480, 2200, 2480],
    { width: CONTENT_WIDTH, borders: tableBorder(BorderStyle.NONE) },
  );

  return [heading, row1, row2];
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Update `buildFooter` to read from metadata instead of `values.identificacao`**

Replace the `buildFooter` function:

```typescript
function buildFooter(runtime: DocumentPayload): Footer {
  const elaboradoPor = runtime.metadata?.elaboradoPor
    ?? (isObject(runtime.values.identificacao) ? asString(runtime.values.identificacao.elaboradoPor) ?? "\u2014" : "\u2014");

  return new Footer({
    children: [
      new Paragraph({
        alignment: AlignmentType.CENTER,
        border: {
          top: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 4, color: C.grayMid },
        },
        children: [
          run(`Elaborado por: ${elaboradoPor}  |  P\u00E1gina `, {
            font: DEFAULT_FONT,
            size: DEFAULT_FONT_SIZE,
          }),
          ...(makePageNumberField() as any[]),
        ],
      }),
    ],
  });
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Wire Section 1 into `generateDocx`**

Update the `children` array:

```typescript
children: [
  buildHeader(runtime),
  ...buildIdentificationSection(runtime),
  ...runtime.schema.sections.flatMap((section) => renderSection(section, runtime.values[section.key] ?? {})),
],
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Verify docgen compiles**

Run: `cd apps/docgen && npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: No errors.

```bash
git add apps/docgen/src/generate.ts
git commit -m "feat(docgen): render auto-generated Section 1 and update footer to use metadata"
```

### Task 7: Docgen — Render auto-generated Section 10 (Histórico de Revisões)

**Files:**
- Modify: `apps/docgen/src/generate.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add `buildRevisionHistorySection` function**

Add after `buildIdentificationSection`:

```typescript
function buildRevisionHistorySection(runtime: DocumentPayload): (Paragraph | Table)[] {
  const revisions = runtime.revisions;
  if (!revisions || revisions.length === 0) return [];

  const sectionColor = C.gray;
  const lightColor = C.grayLight;
  const colWidths = [1400, 1800, 4360, 1800];

  const heading = makeTable(
    [
      new TableRow({
        children: [
          makeCell({
            width: CONTENT_WIDTH,
            fill: sectionColor,
            children: [
              paragraph([run("10  \u2014  HIST\u00D3RICO DE REVIS\u00D5ES", { bold: true, color: C.white })]),
            ],
          }),
        ],
      }),
    ],
    [CONTENT_WIDTH],
    { width: CONTENT_WIDTH, borders: tableBorder(BorderStyle.NONE) },
  );

  const headerRow = new TableRow({
    children: [
      makeCell({ width: colWidths[0], fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Vers\u00E3o", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
      makeCell({ width: colWidths[1], fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Data", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
      makeCell({ width: colWidths[2], fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("O que foi alterado", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
      makeCell({ width: colWidths[3], fill: lightColor, children: [paragraph([run("Por", { bold: true, color: sectionColor })])] }),
    ],
  });

  const dataRows = revisions.map(
    (rev) =>
      new TableRow({
        children: [
          makeCell({ width: colWidths[0], children: [paragraph([run(rev.versao)])] }),
          makeCell({ width: colWidths[1], children: [paragraph([run(rev.data)])] }),
          makeCell({ width: colWidths[2], children: [paragraph([run(rev.descricao)])] }),
          makeCell({ width: colWidths[3], children: [paragraph([run(rev.por)])] }),
        ],
      }),
  );

  const table = makeTable(
    [headerRow, ...dataRows],
    colWidths,
    { width: CONTENT_WIDTH, borders: tableBorder(BorderStyle.NONE) },
  );

  return [heading, table];
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Wire Section 10 into `generateDocx` and update `normalizeDocumentPayload`**

Update the `children` array in `generateDocx`:

```typescript
children: [
  buildHeader(runtime),
  ...buildIdentificationSection(runtime),
  ...runtime.schema.sections.flatMap((section) => renderSection(section, runtime.values[section.key] ?? {})),
  ...buildRevisionHistorySection(runtime),
],
```

In `normalizeDocumentPayload`, add `metadata` and `revisions` to the returned payload object:

```typescript
const payload = {
  documentType,
  documentCode,
  title,
  version: typeof version === "string" && version.trim() ? version.trim() : undefined,
  status: typeof status === "string" && status.trim() ? status.trim() : undefined,
  schema: { sections: schema.sections as DocumentTypeSchema["sections"] },
  values: values as DocumentValues,
  metadata: isObject(input.metadata) ? input.metadata as DocumentPayload["metadata"] : undefined,
  revisions: Array.isArray(input.revisions) ? input.revisions as DocumentPayload["revisions"] : undefined,
} satisfies DocumentPayload;
```

Expected: No errors.

git commit -m "feat(docgen): render auto-generated Section 10 (Histórico de Revisões)"

### Task 8: End-to-end smoke test

- [ ] **Step 1: Rebuild and restart all services**

Run: `docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml down && docker compose -f deploy/compose/docker-compose.yml up -d --build`

Wait for all services to be healthy.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify the schema is served to the content builder**

Run: `curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/v1/document-profiles/po/schemas | python3 -m json.tool | head -30`

Expected: The response `items[0].contentSchema.sections` should contain the 8 new sections (starting with `identificacaoProcesso`), NOT the old Carbone-era sections.

- [ ] **Step 3: Create a test document and verify the editor**

1. Open `http://127.0.0.1:4173`
2. Create a new PO document
3. Navigate to the content builder
4. Verify: 8 sections appear (Identificação do Processo through Glossário)
5. Verify: Section 5 etapas repeat field works (add item, fill rich text)
6. Verify: Section 4 has the rich "Diagrama" field

- [ ] **Step 4: Test DOCX export with content assertions**

1. Fill in test data: at minimum fill `objetivo` in Section 2, add one etapa in Section 5
2. Click "Exportar .docx"
3. Open the downloaded DOCX and verify these specific assertions:

**Header assertions:**
- Row 1 shows "PROCEDIMENTO OPERACIONAL", document code, and version number
- Row 2 shows document type and status

**Section 1 (Identificação) assertions:**
- "IDENTIFICAÇÃO" heading appears after the header
- "Elaborado por" cell shows the document owner's user ID (not empty, not "—" unless no owner)
- "Aprovado por" shows "—" (no approvals yet for a new document)
- "Data de criação" shows today's date (YYYY-MM-DD format)
- "Data de aprovação" shows "—"

**Schema sections (2-9) assertions:**
- Section 2 "Identificação do Processo" appears with the filled `objetivo`
- Section 5 "Detalhamento das Etapas" shows the added etapa
- Sections appear in order 2→3→4→5→6→7→8→9
- No duplicate Section 1 or Section 10 within the schema sections range

**Section 10 (Histórico de Revisões) assertions:**
- "HISTÓRICO DE REVISÕES" heading appears after Section 9
- Table has columns: Versão, Data, O que foi alterado, Por
- At least one row with version "1", today's date, "Criação do documento"

**Footer assertions:**
- Footer shows "Elaborado por: [owner ID] | Página [number]"

- [ ] **Step 5: Test fallback behavior (no approvals, no user resolver)**

Since `WithUserResolver` is not wired, verify:
- Owner ID appears as raw user ID (e.g. "admin" or UUID), not a display name — this is expected
- "Aprovado por" shows "—" — no approvals exist
- Document generates without errors

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit any fixes**

If any issues were found during testing, fix them and commit.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "fix: address issues found during PO schema smoke test"
```
